topic: An employee is transferred to a different department
raw_output:
[story 1]

The memo landed on Sarah’s desk like a lead weight. Another transfer. To Marketing. She stared at the glossy paper, the edges blurring slightly. Her jaw tightened until a dull ache radiated across her temples. "Marketing," she muttered, the word tasting like ash. She picked up her pen, but the ink seemed to swim, refusing to form a coherent line. Every polite smile she practiced for the inevitable meeting felt like a grotesque performance. She paced the cramped cubicle, the cheap carpet seeming to absorb the frantic energy thrumming beneath her skin. *This isn't fair. After all the late nights, the projects I spearheaded... they just want to shuffle me off.* She slammed her hand lightly on the partition, the sound swallowed instantly by the humming of the office machinery.

[story 2]

"So, this is it?" Mark asked, his voice dangerously level as he slid the HR packet across the desk. He didn't look up, his focus fixed on a smudge on his monitor. I felt a sudden, sharp clenching in my chest, like a fist squeezing the air from my lungs. I snatched the papers, my fingers trembling just enough to make the edges crinkle. I didn't bother reading the details; I just needed to get out. I shoved the folder into my bag with unnecessary force, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet corner of the bullpen. When I finally turned, my eyes were cold, hard stones. "Fine," I managed, the word clipped and brittle. "I'll go figure out where I fit in this new circus."

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights of the office seemed to buzz with malicious intent. Elena watched the transfer notification flash on her screen. A slow, hot flush crept up her neck, staining her cheeks a deep crimson. She stood motionless by the window, the city traffic below a meaningless, relentless flow. Her hands, usually so steady when presenting data, were clenched so tightly her knuckles were white peaks against her skin. She walked over to her ergonomic chair and sank into it, letting out a slow, ragged breath that hitched in her throat. *They think this is a minor inconvenience. They think I'll just fold.* A low, simmering resentment began to bubble beneath her calm exterior, a pressure building behind her eyes that demanded an immediate, explosive release.
